title: ochna pygmaea
intro: Ochna pygmaea is a small, deciduous shrub native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems. The health benefits of Ochna pygmaea include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, fever, and wounds. Ochna pygmaea is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical application, and its leaves can be dried and used as a herbal remedy.
